channel.title: Art on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Art on emsenn.net

item.title: Cubism (Design)
item.description:
Cubism, as drawn on by visual engineering practices, is treated not as a “cubist look” to replicate but as a set of tactics for resisting single-view closure and forcing relational seeing. The point is cubism as anti-settling mechanism: keep multiple readings live long enough for attention to move.

Methods and approach

Guillaume Apollinaire’s The Cubist Painters [@apollinaire1913] articulated the movement’s commitment to simultaneous viewpoints — presenting multiple partial views that do not fuse into a single stable depiction immediately. In design terms, this translates into multi-frame layout, engineered discontinuity, and productive ambiguity.
